economic_notes: First Nation has 51% interest in Helios Whitefish River Limited Partnership and 16.55% interest in Mnidoo Mnising Power Limited Partnership (both accounted for by portfolio method). Participates in hotel and conference centre on Manitoulin Island via 7043821 Canada Inc. and Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership. Whitefish River Development Corporation and Birch Island Construction Company fully consolidated.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to non-compliance with PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations (management has not completed assessment of tangible capital assets for potential asset retirement obligations). First Nation also continued to follow PS 3270 Solid Waste Landfill Closure and Post-Closure Liabilities which was no longer in effect. Cash increased significantly from $10.16M to $21.55M largely due to $8.94M increase in deferred revenue. Deferred revenue jumped from $4.55M to $13.49M. Due (to) from Aboriginal Affairs is a negative revenue line of ($1,212,500) included in 2023 revenue total. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + Ontario + Anishinabek organizations = $8,231,897 + $386,983 + $1,551,548 + $3,581,971 + $198,029 (Health Canada balance portion) = estimated; using sum of identifiable government sources. OSR includes interest, user fees, OFNLP less the Aboriginal Affairs adjustment.
